title: SVM, kNN, Naive Bayes + DSA Linked List
focus: Kernel trick, curse of dimensionality, generative vs discriminative.

items.label: Linked List Cycle
interviewQuestions: Walk through Floyd's tortoise & hare — why must they meet inside the cycle?
How do you find the start of the cycle (LC 142) once you've detected one?

items.label: Find The Duplicate Number
interviewQuestions: Walk through your pointer hazards — what breaks if you lose track of the head or a prev pointer?
Can you do this in-place (O(1) extra space)? What's the trick?
How would you detect / handle a cycle, and prove your method's correctness?

items.label: SVM: max-margin, kernel trick, soft margin
interviewQuestions: What's the geometric intuition behind the max-margin SVM?
Explain the kernel trick — why it lets us work in infinite-dimensional spaces without computing them.
When would you NOT use an SVM today?

items.label: kNN: distance metrics, curse of dimensionality
interviewQuestions: Why does kNN degrade in high dimensions (curse of dimensionality)?
How would you choose k and the distance metric for kNN?

items.label: Naive Bayes: when 'naive' actually works
interviewQuestions: Why does Naive Bayes still work despite the unrealistic independence assumption?
Compare generative vs discriminative classifiers using NB and Logistic Regression.
